titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 601

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=910 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:22:01.933152+00 | translation moa=911 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 16:22:01.946777+00

p.cl: W . Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus tocius terre sue . Clericis et laicis salutem .

Sciant presentes . et
futuri me concessisse . et hac Carta mea confirmasse deo . et sancto Cudberto . et Monachis eius
de Dunelmo donacionem Gospatrici comitis . et successorum eius . scilicet Nesebite . et uillam de Ederam
et ecclesiam eiusdem uille . cum omnibus capellis . et iustis pertinenciis suis . in liberam et perpetuam elemo
sinam tenendam ita libere . et quiete . plenarie . et honorifice  sicut carte comitis Gospatr’ . et succes
sorum eius testantur . et confirmant .

Testibus . Ricardo episcopo sancti Andree . Nicholao Cancellario . Ricardo capellano
Ricardo de Moreuilla consabulario . Wellmo filio Alani dapifero . Dauid . olifard . Dolfino capellano . Iohanne filio
Ormin . uicecomite de Rokesburgo .
William, king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land, clerk and lay, greeting.

Let present and future know me to have allowed, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God and Saint Cuthbert, and to his monks at Durham, the donation of Cospatric the earl and his successors, that is to say, Nisbet and the toun of Edrom, and the church in the same toun, with all its chapels and pertinents, in free and perpetual alms, to be held as freely and quitly and honourably as the charters of Earl Cospatric and his successors bear witness and affirm.

As witnesses: Richard bishop of St Andrews, Nicholas the chancellor, Richard the chaplain, Richard de Morville the constable, William son of Alan, steward, David Oliphant, Dolphin the chaplain, John son of Orm, sheriff of Roxburgh,
